2026-04-20T11:51:19.5925895Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5927181Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5927643Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5928014Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5928333Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5928578Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5928747Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5928932Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5929106Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5929345Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5929550Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5929839Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-20T11:51:19.5930102Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["issue_number"],"type":"object"},"name":"assign_to_user"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"message":{"description":"The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols","type":"string"},"owners":{"description":"Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')","type":"string"}},"required":["message","owners"],"type":"object"},"name":"mention_owners"}]}}}

{"timestamp":"2026-04-20T11:52:25.214781209Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58383,\"title\":\"[Azure Generator] Docs: Document auto-scaffolding of README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and Directory.Build.props\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp` (Azure Data Plane Generator)\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57653 (commit fe4c0e1e) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57653 updated `NewAzureProjectScaffolding.WriteAdditionalFiles` to automatically generate three scaffolding files when code is first emitted into a directory under `sdk/`:\\n\\n- `README.md` — pre-filled with standard section headers (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing)\\n- `CHANGELOG.md` — pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template\\n- `Directory.Build.props` — pre-filled with the standard import chain for azure-sdk-for-net packages\\n\\nFiles are only written if they do **not** already exist, so customised content is never overwritten.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The emitter README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` has no mention of these auto-generated files or when they are produced\\n- The `new-project` option description only says it overwrites the `.csproj`; there is no explanation that new scaffolding files (README, CHANGELOG, Directory.Build.props) are also created on first use under `sdk/`\\n- There is no note that the scaffolding is intentionally a one-shot operation (files are not overwritten on subsequent regenerations), which is important for users who need to know they can safely customise these files\\n- No guidance on what the generated templates look like or how to extend them via the `GetReadmeContent` / `GetChangelogContent` / `GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent` virtual methods for custom generators\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit — add a new sub-section under `## Emitter options` (after the `new-project` option entry) and a new top-level section for scaffolding behaviour.\\n\\nAdd the following after the `### new-project` option block:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Scaffolded files (new projects under `sdk/`)\\n\\nWhen generating a **new** project whose output directory is located under the `sdk/` directory of the azure-sdk-for-net repository, the generator automatically creates the following files alongside the `.csproj` and `.sln` files — **only if they do not already exist**:\\n\\n| File | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `README.md` | Pre-filled with standard azure-sdk-for-net section headings (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) |\\n| `CHANGELOG.md` | Pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template |\\n| `Directory.Build.props` | Pre-filled with the standard MSBuild import chain required by azure-sdk-for-net |\\n\\nBecause these files are written only once, you can freely customise them after they are generated — subsequent runs of the generator will never overwrite your changes.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** This scaffolding is only triggered when the emitter output directory contains `/sdk/` in its path. Projects outside `sdk/` (for example, spector test projects under `eng/`) do not receive these files.\\n\\n#### Extending scaffolded content in a custom generator\\n\\nIf you have built a custom generator that extends `NewAzureProjectScaffolding`, you can override the following virtual methods to change the generated template content:\\n\\n```csharp\\nprotected virtual string GetReadmeContent(string packageName) { ... }\\nprotected virtual string GetChangelogContent(string packageName) { ... }\\nprotected virtual string GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent(string packageName) { ... }\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation change. The feature is purely generator infrastructure and does not expose a user-facing API surface that would benefit from a runnable sample in a test file.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe existing README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` already contains all required sections. Only the **Emitter options** section needs the new content described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/Azure.Generator.csproj` — verify the generator still builds\\n2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/test/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` — verify tests pass (the new `WriteAdditionalFilesTests.cs` should be included)\\n\\nNo snippet update or API export steps are required because only documentation (Markdown) is being changed.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24664599157/agentic_workflow) · ● 418.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58383\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-20T11:49:44Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-20T11:49:44Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}

Generator] Docs: Document auto-scaffolding of README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and Directory.Build.props\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp` (Azure Data Plane Generator)\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57653 (commit fe4c0e1e) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57653 updated `NewAzureProjectScaffolding.WriteAdditionalFiles` to automatically generate three scaffolding files when code is first emitted into a directory under `sdk/`:\\n\\n- `README.md` — pre-filled with standard section headers (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing)\\n- `CHANGELOG.md` — pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template\\n- `Directory.Build.props` — pre-filled with the standard import chain for azure-sdk-for-net packages\\n\\nFiles are only written if they do **not** already exist, so customised content is never overwritten.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The emitter README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` has no mention of these auto-generated files or when they are produced\\n- The `new-project` option description only says it overwrites the `.csproj`; there is no explanation that new scaffolding files (README, CHANGELOG, Directory.Build.props) are also created on first use under `sdk/`\\n- There is no note that the scaffolding is intentionally a one-shot operation (files are not overwritten on subsequent regenerations), which is important for users who need to know they can safely customise these files\\n- No guidance on what the generated templates look like or how to extend them via the `GetReadmeContent` / `GetChangelogContent` / `GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent` virtual methods for custom generators\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit — add a new sub-section under `## Emitter options` (after the `new-project` option entry) and a new top-level section for scaffolding behaviour.\\n\\nAdd the following after the `### new-project` option block:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Scaffolded files (new projects under `sdk/`)\\n\\nWhen generating a **new** project whose output directory is located under the `sdk/` directory of the azure-sdk-for-net repository, the generator automatically creates the following files alongside the `.csproj` and `.sln` files — **only if they do not already exist**:\\n\\n| File | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `README.md` | Pre-filled with standard azure-sdk-for-net section headings (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) |\\n| `CHANGELOG.md` | Pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template |\\n| `Directory.Build.props` | Pre-filled with the standard MSBuild import chain required by azure-sdk-for-net |\\n\\nBecause these files are written only once, you can freely customise them after they are generated — subsequent runs of the generator will never overwrite your changes.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** This scaffolding is only triggered when the emitter output directory contains `/sdk/` in its path. Projects outside `sdk/` (for example, spector test projects under `eng/`) do not receive these files.\\n\\n#### Extending scaffolded content in a custom generator\\n\\nIf you have built a custom generator that extends `NewAzureProjectScaffolding`, you can override the following virtual methods to change the generated template content:\\n\\n```csharp\\nprotected virtual string GetReadmeContent(string packageName) { ... }\\nprotected virtual string GetChangelogContent(string packageName) { ... }\\nprotected virtual string GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent(string packageName) { ... }\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation change. The feature is purely generator infrastructure and does not expose a user-facing API surface that would benefit from a runnable sample in a test file.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe existing README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` already contains all required sections. Only the **Emitter options** section needs the new content described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/Azure.Generator.csproj` — verify the generator still builds\\n2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/test/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` — verify tests pass (the new `WriteAdditionalFilesTests.cs` should be included)\\n\\nNo snippet update or API export steps are required because only documentation (Markdown) is being changed.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; {"timestamp":"2026-04-20T11:52:58.790674765Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58383,\"title\":\"[Azure Generator] Docs: Document auto-scaffolding of README.md, CHANGELOG.md, and Directory.Build.props\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp` (Azure Data Plane Generator)\\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57653 (commit fe4c0e1e) by `@ArcturusZhang`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57653 updated `NewAzureProjectScaffolding.WriteAdditionalFiles` to automatically generate three scaffolding files when code is first emitted into a directory under `sdk/`:\\n\\n- `README.md` — pre-filled with standard section headers (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing)\\n- `CHANGELOG.md` — pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template\\n- `Directory.Build.props` — pre-filled with the standard import chain for azure-sdk-for-net packages\\n\\nFiles are only written if they do **not** already exist, so customised content is never overwritten.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The emitter README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` has no mention of these auto-generated files or when they are produced\\n- The `new-project` option description only says it overwrites the `.csproj`; there is no explanation that new scaffolding files (README, CHANGELOG, Directory.Build.props) are also created on first use under `sdk/`\\n- There is no note that the scaffolding is intentionally a one-shot operation (files are not overwritten on subsequent regenerations), which is important for users who need to know they can safely customise these files\\n- No guidance on what the generated templates look like or how to extend them via the `GetReadmeContent` / `GetChangelogContent` / `GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent` virtual methods for custom generators\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md`  \\n**Action:** Edit — add a new sub-section under `## Emitter options` (after the `new-project` option entry) and a new top-level section for scaffolding behaviour.\\n\\nAdd the following after the `### new-project` option block:\\n\\n````markdown\\n### Scaffolded files (new projects under `sdk/`)\\n\\nWhen generating a **new** project whose output directory is located under the `sdk/` directory of the azure-sdk-for-net repository, the generator automatically creates the following files alongside the `.csproj` and `.sln` files — **only if they do not already exist**:\\n\\n| File | Description |\\n|---|---|\\n| `README.md` | Pre-filled with standard azure-sdk-for-net section headings (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) |\\n| `CHANGELOG.md` | Pre-filled with the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` release history template |\\n| `Directory.Build.props` | Pre-filled with the standard MSBuild import chain required by azure-sdk-for-net |\\n\\nBecause these files are written only once, you can freely customise them after they are generated — subsequent runs of the generator will never overwrite your changes.\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** This scaffolding is only triggered when the emitter output directory contains `/sdk/` in its path. Projects outside `sdk/` (for example, spector test projects under `eng/`) do not receive these files.\\n\\n#### Extending scaffolded content in a custom generator\\n\\nIf you have built a custom generator that extends `NewAzureProjectScaffolding`, you can override the following virtual methods to change the generated template content:\\n\\n```csharp\\nprotected virtual string GetReadmeContent(string packageName) { ... }\\nprotected virtual string GetChangelogContent(string packageName) { ... }\\nprotected virtual string GetDirectoryBuildPropsContent(string packageName) { ... }\\n```\\n````\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new test-backed snippets are required for this documentation change. The feature is purely generator infrastructure and does not expose a user-facing API surface that would benefit from a runnable sample in a test file.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe existing README at `eng/packages/http-client-csharp/README.md` already contains all required sections. Only the **Emitter options** section needs the new content described in Step 1.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/src/Azure.Generator.csproj` — verify the generator still builds\\n2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp/generator/Azure.Generator/test/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` — verify tests pass (the new `WriteAdditionalFilesTests.cs` should be included)\\n\\nNo snippet update or API export steps are required because only documentation (Markdown) is being changed.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `pkgs.dev.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt; - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;pkgs.dev.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;releaseassets.githubusercontent.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt;
